purpose: Document 8 retained RED tests across 3 technical buckets (Bucket C empty post-verification)
cap_exceeded_rationale: M2 verification surfaced 3 NEW carry-forwards (cpp/java/scala_deserialization_positive) from a distinct technical class (nested-constructor var-extraction in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast) not anticipated in M1 or premortem. Per validator_mandate carry_forward_max_5: surface explicitly with non-additive-resolution rationale. Future var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 milestone closes Bucket B (3 tests); future rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 closes Bucket A Rust subset (4 tests); future ruby-backtick-extraction-v1 closes Bucket A Ruby subset (1 test). Bucket C (M5-surfaced) is empty — no new carry-forwards emerged from M5 verification beyond what M2 introduced.

A_m1_classified.description: Original M1 carry-forwards (premortem-anticipated). 1 Ruby + 4 Rust dispositions.

tests.rationale: Ruby backtick subshell `\`#{cmd}\`` parses as `subshell` node, not `call_expression`. AST shape inexpressible without FP risk in current call_node_kinds(Ruby). References FAI-v1 `\bgets\b` precedent verbatim — same shape of carry-forward (raw-substring/AST-node mismatch documented as out-of-scope).

analyze_rust_file dispatch bypass per M1 finding. CLI commands/remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 routes .rs files exclusively to analyze_rust_file (UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic line scanner) bypassing scan_vulnerabilities (canonical taint pipeline). Reframe-C carry-forward: requires design milestone for Rust vuln-taint pipeline integration.
Same root cause as rust_command_injection_positive — analyze_rust_file dispatch bypass at remaining/vuln.rs:368-370.
Same root cause as rust_command_injection_positive — analyze_rust_file dispatch bypass at remaining/vuln.rs:368-370.
Same root cause as rust_command_injection_positive — analyze_rust_file dispatch bypass at remaining/vuln.rs:368-370.

B_m2_surfaced_var_extraction.description: M2-surfaced carry-forwards from a distinct technical class — extract_first_identifier_arg_ast cannot descend through nested constructors (object_creation / new_expression) to reach the underlying identifier, returning var=None and short-circuiting taint propagation. Distinct from Bucket A (no analyze_*_file dispatch analog for these langs; canonical compute_taint_with_tree DOES run on .cpp / .java / .scala).

tests.rationale: tree-sitter-cpp parsing variance on `boost::archive::text_iarchive` constructor declaration shape. Bank entry exists but doesn't fire empirically. Root cause likely the constructor-declaration node shape differing from member-call expectations in the AST sink-pattern matcher.

extract_first_identifier_arg_ast returns var=None because first arg to ObjectInputStream constructor is `new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes())` (nested constructor, not identifier). Var-extraction logic doesn't descend through `object_creation` / `new_expression` nodes. Out of M2 additive scope (would require new code path, not bank entry).
Same root cause as java_deserialization_positive — Scala uses java's ObjectInputStream constructor in nested-construction shape. Same var-extraction limitation applies.

C_m5_surfaced.description: Reserved for any new carry-forwards surfaced during M5 final verification. Empty post-verification — no new technical classes emerged.

follow_on_milestones.name: var-extract-nested-constructor-v1
follow_on_milestones.scope: Extend extract_first_identifier_arg_ast to descend through object_creation / new_expression / nested-call nodes when extracting var from a constructor argument. Closes 3 carry-forwards (Bucket B): cpp/java/scala_deserialization_positive.

follow_on_milestones.name: rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1
follow_on_milestones.scope: Design how analyze_rust_file (UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic line scanner at remaining/vuln.rs) interacts with scan_vulnerabilities (canonical taint pipeline) for Rust. Currently CLI's analyze_file at remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 routes .rs exclusively to analyze_rust_file. Two options: (a) wire scan_vulnerabilities through to .rs alongside analyze_rust_file with output merging; (b) port scan_vulnerabilities's taint detection into analyze_rust_file natively. Closes 4 carry-forwards (Bucket A Rust subset).
follow_on_milestones.loc_estimate: TBD; design milestone first then implementation milestone

follow_on_milestones.name: ruby-backtick-extraction-v1
follow_on_milestones.scope: Add Ruby tree-sitter `subshell` node kind to call_node_kinds(Ruby) OR add a new dispatch path that handles Ruby backtick `subshell` nodes as ShellExec sinks. Closes 1 carry-forward (Bucket A Ruby subset).

field_access_info-extension-v1.rationale: tree-sitter-ruby parses bare `gets` as identifier, not `call` node. AST `call_names: ['gets']` does not cover bare-call form. Documented as RETAINED exception in RUBY_AST_SOURCES regex bank deletion atomic milestone.
field_access_info-extension-v1.applied_here: ruby_command_injection_positive (Bucket A) follows the same shape — Ruby AST parser produces a non-call node kind that current dispatch cannot cover without false-positive risk.
